title: Container Packaging, Startup, Health, Migration Coordination, and Rollout · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent university archive receives a different publisher mix, unknown XML shape, validator state, tenant, async schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers container packaging, startup, health, migration coordination, and rollout into a changed deployable emitted TypeScript feed service. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of deployable emitted TypeScript feed service with automated and judgment evidence. Le…
objectives: Inspect packed ESM exports, emitted JavaScript, maps, production dependencies, migrations, licenses, and startup command in a clean Node 24 environment.
Build a pinned non-root minimal image with exact package, certificates, ownership, metadata, SBOM, signature, and vulnerability evidence.
Validate configuration, dependency compatibility, migration state, secrets, budgets, and separate startup, liveness, readiness, degradation, and maintenance semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Package Artifact
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 1021 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bind package, non-root container, health, migration ownership, compatibility, canary, and rollback evidence; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, migrations, lockfile, package, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect packed ESM exports, emitted JavaScript, maps, production dependencies, migrations, licenses, and startup command in a clean Node 24 environment.

What must be established before implementation?
